title: Analytics Forecaster EA
subtitle: Analytics Master EA + Telegram Bot Integration — Live Market Alerts to Mobile
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: BUILD — extract the NotificationService as a standalone broker-agnostic component

series_evolution.part_3: EA — live dashboard, auto-refreshes every 2 hours
series_evolution.part_4: EA — Part 3 + Telegram push notification on each refresh cycle
series_evolution.key_addition: One new function (SendTelegramMessage), three new config params. Every other line of code is identical to Part 3. This part is entirely about the notification transport layer.

tool_classification.notable: Version string bumped to 1.1. Author's tool changelog now refers to this as 'Third Edition' despite being Part 4 — the numbering in the changelog is off by one.

depends_on.new_vs_part_3: Only NotificationService is new. All analysis logic is identical to Part 3.

new_config_params.name: TelegramToken
new_config_params.description: The Telegram Bot API token obtained from BotFather. Required to authenticate HTTP requests to the Telegram API.

new_config_params.name: ChatID
new_config_params.description: The Telegram chat ID where messages will be delivered. Can be a personal chat or group chat ID.

new_config_params.name: SendTelegramAlerts
new_config_params.description: Master on/off switch for Telegram notifications. Allows disabling alerts without code changes.

new_function.name: SendTelegramMessage
new_function.error_handling: Prints HTTP response code and last error on failure

new_output_field.name: pair_name
new_output_field.description: Current instrument name prepended to the message so Telegram recipient knows which pair the data is for
new_output_field.note: Part 3 did not include the pair name in the dashboard. Part 4 adds it because the Telegram message has no chart context — the reader needs to know which symbol it relates to.

telegram_setup_steps.step_4: Choose bot name and username (must end in 'bot')
telegram_setup_steps.step_6_get_chat_id_method_1: Start @get_id_bot or @userinfobot — they reply with your chat ID
telegram_setup_steps.step_6_get_chat_id_method_2: Send any message to your bot, then visit https://api.telegram.org/bot{TOKEN}/getUpdates — chat_id is in the result JSON
telegram_setup_steps.mt5_prerequisite: In MT5: Tools > Options > Expert Advisors > Allow WebRequest for https://api.telegram.org

http_request_breakdown.response_check: HTTP 200 = success. Anything else = log error code.
http_request_breakdown.critical_security_note: The bot token is stored as a plain-text input parameter in the EA settings. Anyone with access to the EA settings file or strategy tester log can see the token. Our build must offer secure token storage — environment variable or encrypted config file.
http_request_breakdown.json_injection_risk: The message string is inserted directly into JSON without escaping. If the infoStr contains special characters like quotes, backslashes, or newlines it will break the JSON payload. Our NotificationService must sanitise the message before embedding it.
http_request_breakdown.rate_limit: Telegram Bot API limits: 1 message per second to the same chat, 30 messages per second across all chats. The 2-hour refresh interval is well within limits.

data_requirements: Identical to Part 3 — see part_03.json for full data requirements spec

telegram_token.description: Bot API token from BotFather — MUST be kept secret

chat_id.description: Telegram chat or group ID — destination for alerts

send_telegram_alerts.description: Master switch — disable without code changes

notification_logic.trigger: Every 2-hour refresh cycle — same gate as Part 3
notification_logic.content: Full 18-field infoStr including pair name, all metrics, last update timestamp
sequence: 2. Format infoStr (add pair name at top vs Part 3)
5. If SendTelegramAlerts: POST to Telegram API

components_to_build.name: NotificationService

contract.send: send(channel: str, message: str, metadata: dict) -> bool

TelegramChannel.auth: bot_token from secure config (NOT plain-text input param)

implementations.WebhookChannel: POST JSON payload to any URL — for Slack, Discord, custom endpoints
implementations.EmailChannel: SMTP or sendgrid — for end-of-day summary emails
implementations.SoundChannel: Play local audio file — for alerts when away from screen

components_to_build.security: Token stored in environment variable or encrypted config — never in plain text

message_formatting.sanitise: Escape JSON special chars: backslash, quote, newline, tab before embedding in payload
message_formatting.markdown_support: Telegram supports Markdown in messages — use *bold* for direction signal, `code` for prices

security_issues.description: TelegramToken stored as an MQL5 input string — visible in EA properties dialog, strategy tester logs, and exported .set files
security_issues.fix: Store token in a separate encrypted config file or environment variable. Never in source code or user-facing settings.

security_issues.issue: JSON injection via unescaped message content
security_issues.description: infoStr is embedded directly into the JSON payload string without escaping. Newlines (\n) in the message will break the JSON unless the implementation handles this correctly.
security_issues.fix: Use a proper JSON serialiser. Escape all special characters in the message string before embedding.

security_issues.description: If the HTTP request fails (timeout, network error), the message is lost with no retry. The EA continues silently.
security_issues.fix: Queue failed messages and retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff.

keep: The Telegram integration concept — mobile push alerts for trading tools is genuinely valuable
The SendTelegramAlerts toggle — clean on/off without code changes
Adding pair name to the message — essential context for mobile reading
The HTTP POST approach — simple, reliable, supported by all Telegram bot implementations
Treating notification as a separate function (SendTelegramMessage) — correct separation of concerns
discard: Plain-text token as input parameter — serious security risk
Direct string embedding in JSON payload — replace with proper JSON serialisation
No retry on HTTP failure — implement queued retry
MQL5 WebRequest API — replaced by our HTTP client in NotificationService
improve: Secure token storage (env var or encrypted config file)
Telegram Markdown formatting: bold direction signal, monospace prices
Message deduplication: don't send if content is identical to last message
Multi-channel support: same message to Telegram AND webhook AND sound
Alert throttling: if Telegram fails 3 times, disable and log — don't spam error logs
Selective alerting: config option to only send Telegram if direction changes (not every 2 hours)
Message queue: if market is closed or network is down, queue and send on reconnect

article_figures.title: Part 3 Analytics Master Result (recap)
article_figures.insight: Shows the Part 3 dashboard used as the baseline. Confirms the data panel content that Part 4 forwards to Telegram.

article_figures.title: Integration Pathway Diagram
article_figures.insight: Architecture diagram: MT5 EA → HTTP POST → Telegram Bot API → Telegram Chat. Simple three-hop integration.

article_figures.title: BotFather in Telegram
article_figures.insight: Screenshot of BotFather search result in Telegram app. Step 2 of bot creation.

article_figures.title: Start Chat with BotFather
article_figures.insight: Steps 3 and 4 combined — start chat and create new bot via /newbot command.

article_figures.title: Receive Bot Token
article_figures.insight: BotFather response showing the bot token. The token is the string after 'Use this token to access the HTTP API:'. Format: {numbers}:{alphanumeric string}.

article_figures.title: Get Chat ID via @userinfobot
article_figures.insight: Shows @userinfobot response — returns your numeric Telegram user ID which is also your personal chat ID.

article_figures.title: Telegram Message with Pair Name
article_figures.insight: The actual Telegram message received. Shows all 18 fields including Pair: USDSEK at top. Confirms the plain-text format — no formatting, just newline-separated key:value pairs.

article_figures.title: Telegram Result GIF
article_figures.insight: Animated — shows messages arriving in the Telegram chat in real time as the EA sends them. Confirms the live integration is working end-to-end.

article_figures.title: MT5 Chart vs Telegram Side-by-Side
article_figures.insight: Most important screenshot — shows the MT5 chart data panel on the left and the identical information in Telegram on the right. Proves the data integrity of the integration.
